overview: The fuel code accounts for the difference in fuel between last OFP Block Fuel received by the crew, and the actual fuel on-board at Chocks-Off. All Fuel codes are used at Departure. For each code used, a reason/comment must be entered in FOL remarks.

naming_convention.first_letter: Phase of flight: D=Departure, E=En-route, A=Arrival (except Group 3)

reason_codes.FOB: Fuel on Board from previous sector
reason_codes.SCF: Increase Statistical Contingency Fuel from 95% to 99%

groups.name: Group 1 – Departure
groups.description: Standard departure fuel codes with phase prefix D (Departure)

codes.description: Fuel on board from previous sector
MEL items not included in OFP
Drop or increase in ZFW at departure
Increase Statistical Contingency Fuel from 95% to 99%

groups.name: Group 2 – En-route / Arrival
groups.description: En-route (E) and Arrival (A) fuel codes. ZFW, MEL, and SCF codes are not applicable for en-route/arrival.

groups.name: Group 3 – Departure Only (Special)
groups.description: New special fuel codes used at Departure ONLY. These do not follow the phase-prefix naming convention.

codes.description: Compensate for unplanned APU fuel burn
Fuel values changes in latest OFP not associated with ZFW (i.e. last minute changes to flight plan)
Additional fuel uplift strictly used when a designated VVIP on-board

examples.scenario: AUH departure runway change from 31L to 13R
Remaining fuel from previous tankering flight
Additional APU burn due to slot allocation beyond STD
examples.comment: ADD APU BURN DUE TO SLOT

notes: The figure may be positive or negative
Positive (+): Actual fuel more than fuel on latest OFP version
Negative (-): Actual fuel less than fuel on latest OFP version
Example: DZFW -0.5 ton: ZFW drop resulting in 500KGs less uplift
Codes and uplifted fuel quantities must also be annotated on the OFP
